channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Remote work -- not AI -- has sidelined recent college graduates, research finds
item.description: Research from the New York Fed finds that younger college graduates have been sidelined by remote work in recent years, as companies may be reluctant to hire those needing more training and mentoring.
item.content:encoded:
Research from the New York Fed finds that younger college graduates have been sidelined by remote work in recent years, as companies may be reluctant to hire those needing more training and mentoring.

(Image credit: Maksym Belchenko)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: A cancer vaccine made just for you. mRNA is back and it's fighting melanoma
item.description: A study finds that an mRNA vaccine is highly effective at preventing recurrence of this dangerous skin cancer, when used in combination with Keytruda, an immunotherapy drug.
item.content:encoded: A study finds that an mRNA vaccine is highly effective at preventing recurrence of this dangerous skin cancer, when used in combination with Keytruda, an immunotherapy drug.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: U.S. strikes Iranian military sites. And, Trump's $1.8 billion fund faces scrutiny
item.description: American aircraft fired on a number of Iranian sites over the weekend, including Qeeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz. And, Trump's anti-weaponization fund faces scrutiny in Congress and the courts.
item.content:encoded:
American aircraft fired on a number of Iranian sites over the weekend, including Qeeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz. And, Trump's anti-weaponization fund faces scrutiny in Congress and the courts.

(Image credit: Kawnat Haju)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo reach 282 as survivors describe their recoveries
item.description: The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.
item.content:encoded:
The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Is bovine colostrum really 'liquid gold' for gut health?
item.description: Baby calves rely on it to build up their immune systems and gut. And now marketers are promoting it for humans. Here's what scientists say.
item.content:encoded: Baby calves rely on it to build up their immune systems and gut. And now marketers are promoting it for humans. Here's what scientists say.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: 'At what point does it make sense to ditch a gas car for an electric vehicle?'
item.description: "At what point does it make sense to ditch a gas car for an EV?" NPR listener Guadalupe Higuera of Phoenix asked this question and worked with Climate Desk reporter Jeff Brady to answer it.
item.content:encoded:
"At what point does it make sense to ditch a gas car for an EV?" NPR listener Guadalupe Higuera of Phoenix asked this question and worked with Climate Desk reporter Jeff Brady to answer it.

(Image credit: Jeff Brady)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady

item.title: One by one, U.S. civil rights agency dismantles tools to fight discrimination
item.description: The EEOC is seeking to overturn rules created decades ago to tackle discrimination in employment. The Trump administration says those rules have led to more discrimination —against white people.
item.content:encoded:
The EEOC is seeking to overturn rules created decades ago to tackle discrimination in employment. The Trump administration says those rules have led to more discrimination —against white people.

(Image credit: Afro American Newspapers/Gado/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel expands Lebanon offensive as U.S.-Iran peace talks stall, Congress returns to D.C. with long to-do list, rulings create more obstacles for Trump's 'anti-weaponization' fund.
item.content:encoded: Israel expands Lebanon offensive as U.S.-Iran peace talks stall, Congress returns to D.C. with long to-do list, rulings create more obstacles for Trump's 'anti-weaponization' fund.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Violent clashes give way to peaceful PSG parade after Champions League win
item.description: A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.
item.content:encoded:
A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.

(Image credit: Emma Da Silva)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Right-wing candidate pulls ahead in first round of Colombia's presidential vote
item.description: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.
item.content:encoded:
Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.

(Image credit: Fernando Vergara)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
